sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed after MOUSE
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation['nodes']
after = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']

def get_by_color(nodes, color):
    results = []
    for n in nodes:
        if n['color'] == color:
            rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
            cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
            results.append((min(rows), max(rows), min(cols), max(cols), n['pixels']))
    return results

for color in ['N', 'b', 'Y', 'c', 'M']:
    b = get_by_color(before, color)
    a = get_by_color(after, color)
    if b != a:
        print(f"{color}: {b} -> {a}")
    else:
        print(f"{color}: unchanged")

# Maybe I need to rethink the goal. Let me look at the initial frame to understand the setup better.
# Let me check the very first frame in history
if history:
    first_seg = history[0].frame.segmentation['nodes']
    print("\n--- Initial frame objects ---")
    for n in first_seg:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        print(f"  {n['color']}: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}, pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
